pages.raw_text:
The meeting of the Central La
Dor union last night was decided
ly tame. Any one who did not
know that the body was a labor
organization would have had diff
Guilty in deciding whether the
meeting was for the purpose of
studying political economy or was
a fraternal society.


The BARRIERS reported the Vic
toria Hotel, BensOn's and the O. K.
shops as being unfair to their
union. The electrical workers re-
minded the delegates present that
the Washington Water Power Co.
was still at war with union labor.
The cooks and kitchen helpers
stated that the WOman's Exchange
was employing a full crew of
Chinese in the kitchen and was a
place to be avoided by all friends
of white labor, irrespective of their
affiliation with a labor union. The
bartenders reported more trouble
with Kronenberg Bros. 1t was
stated that the central body had
one Of the saloons owned by these
people on the unfair list, and the
other one, located under the Ex-
change bank, had gone bad. The
central body was asked to place
the house on the unfair list.


| The central body decided to hold
an open meeting on the fifth Mon
day in every month. A committee
Of five were appointed to arrange
a program and the meeting will be
open to all, union or nOn-union; in
fact an effort will be made to have
as many nCn-uniOn people attend
as possible. in order that they may


better understand the objects 0
organized labor. 1t was suggested


that speakers be secured to lee
ture on these occasions, and if tht
central body wishes to discuss


polite it can do so at these meet
ings.
